pattern: Replay the operation log on a stack, pushing literals, derived sums and doubles, or popping on cancel, then total the surviving entries.
hints: A stack naturally models a record where the newest score is always the one referenced by the next operation.
Each token either pushes a number, pushes the sum of the top two, pushes double the top, or pops the top.
After processing every operation, the answer is simply the sum of whatever remains on the stack.

edge_inputs.description: mix of literal, cancel, double, and sum
cancel empties the record back to zero
double then cancel removes only the latest
repeated sums build on each other

stress.description: valid random operation sequences honoring the preconditions for +, D, and C
stress.note: values kept within the stated 32-bit-safe range
